Create a Blazor Web App for a university course catalog.

Requirements:
- Browse departments and courses in a searchable, filterable list
- View course detail pages (description, schedule, credits, instructor bio)
- Students submit an enrollment request form (name, student ID, course selection) — standard HTML form, no wizards or real-time validation
- Homepage shows featured courses and department links
- No authentication — open to all students and visitors
- Hosted on the university intranet with fast, reliable connectivity

**Structure:**
- `Models/` — `Course`, `Department`, `EnrollmentRequest` (with validation attributes)
- `Services/CatalogService` — in-memory data with search/filter methods
- **Pages:**
  - `/` — Homepage with featured courses + department links
  - `/courses` — Searchable, filterable course list (query string-based filtering)
  - `/courses/{id}` — Course detail (description, schedule, credits, instructor bio)
  - `/enroll` — Enrollment request form (standard HTML form with antiforgery)

**Key decisions:**
- Static SSR (no WebSocket/SignalR) — ideal for intranet with fast connectivity
- No authentication as specified
- Standard HTML form POST for enrollment (no wizard/real-time validation)
- `CatalogService` registered as singleton (swap for EF Core/DB later)

Run with `dotnet run` from the `UniversityCourseCatalog/` directory.
